# UC-22.14.42 · Information Management — Sensitive Fields in Unapproved Indexes

## Description

Scans high-volume indexes for patterns resembling government IDs or payment cards to support SI-12 information management and handling.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes app and sourcetypes app:json. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Run from isolated role with limited indexes; (2) mask at ingest after discovery; (3) never broadly expose results; (4) document data handling per SI-12 procedure. Indexes required: `index=app`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=app:json`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Processor (Splunk Cloud Platform), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=*` excluding known-safe indexes — run in restricted search mode.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Run from isolated role with limited indexes; (2) mask at ingest after discovery; (3) never broadly expose results; (4) document data handling per SI-12 procedure.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=app sourcetype=app:json earliest=-24h
| regex _raw="(?i)\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?)\b"
| stats count by index, sourcetype, host
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Information Management — Sensitive Fields in Unapproved Indexes (SI-12)** — Scans high-volume indexes for patterns resembling government IDs or payment cards to support SI-12 information management and handling.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=*` excluding known-safe indexes — run in restricted search mode. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Processor (Splunk Cloud Platform), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: app; **sourcetype**: app:json. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=app, sourcetype=app:json, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters rows matching a pattern with `regex`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by index, sourcetype, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hits by index), Single value (unique hosts)

## SPL

```spl
index=app sourcetype=app:json earliest=-24h
| regex _raw="(?i)\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?)\b"
| stats count by index, sourcetype, host
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (hits by index), Single value (unique hosts)
